## Goal 1: Identify the first place mentioned by name in the Book of Esther (New International Version)
- Path 1.1: Search for the exact text of Esther 1:1-2 from the NIV using a web search query like “Esther 1:1-2 NIV text”
  - Success: Retrieved the verses and identified the first proper place name that appears in the translation.
- Path 1.2: Crawl a reliable Bible website (e.g., BibleGateway.com) using the URL “https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Esther+1%3A1-2&version=NIV” and extract all place names mentioned in order.
  - Success: Extracted the full passage and listed “India”, “Cush”, and “Susa” in order of appearance.
- Path 1.3: Search for commentary or analysis that explicitly states “the first place mentioned in the Book of Esther” to confirm the name without ambiguity.
  - Success: Found a trusted source (e.g., Bible study site) that identifies the first named location.

## Goal 2: Determine the modern-day country/region of that place and its head of government title in April 1977
- Path 2.1: If the first place is “India”, search for “Government of India April 1977 head of government” to confirm the position title (Prime Minister) and the historical context.
  - Success: Established that India’s head of government is the Prime Minister and located records for April 1977.
- Path 2.2: If the first place is “Susa”, search for “Susa modern location” to determine its country (Iran), then search for “Iran April 1977 head of government” to see if a Prime Minister existed (yes, under the monarchy).
  - Success: Identified Iran’s head of government as the Prime Minister in April 1977.
- Path 2.3: If the first place is “Cush”, search for “Cush modern identification” (commonly linked to Sudan or Ethiopia), then verify the head of government structure for that country in April 1977 (e.g., Sudan had a President, Ethiopia had no PM).
  - Success: Clarified whether a Prime Minister position existed in that country at the time.

## Goal 3: Identify the specific individual who served as Prime Minister of that place in April 1977
- Path 3.1: If the place is India, search for “Prime Minister of India April 1977” and cross-reference with historical election dates (e.g., Morarji Desai took office on 24 March 1977).
  - Success: Confirmed name and exact date range covering April 1977.
- Path 3.2: If the place is Iran, search for “Prime Minister of Iran April 1977” (e.g., Amir-Abbas Hoveyda was in office until August 1977).
  - Success: Verified tenure via multiple historical sources (e.g., Wikipedia, government archives).
- Path 3.3: If the place is a country with a different title (e.g., Sudan or Ethiopia), search for “head of state [country] April 1977” and check if a Prime Minister existed or if the leader held another title (e.g., President, Chairman). If no Prime Minister, this path would need reconsideration of the place name.
  - Success: Established that the country either had a Prime Minister or that the first place identified leads to a different interpretation (fallback to Goal 1).

- Path 1.2: Crawl a reliable Bible website (e.g., BibleGateway.com) using the URL “https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Esther+1%3A1-2&version=NIV” and extract all place names mentioned in order.
  - Success: Extracted the full passage and listed “India”, “Cush”, and “Susa” in order of appearance.
- Path 1.3: Search for commentary or analysis that explicitly states “the first place mentioned in the Book of Esther” to confirm the name without ambiguity.
  - Success: Found a trusted source (e.g., Bible study site) that identifies the first named location.

## Goal 2: Determine the modern-day country/region of that place and its head of government title in April 1977
- Path 2.1: If the first place is “India”, search for “Government of India April 1977 head of government” to confirm the position title (Prime Minister) and the historical context.

- Path 2.3: If the first place is “Cush”, search for “Cush modern identification” (commonly linked to Sudan or Ethiopia), then verify the head of government structure for that country in April 1977 (e.g., Sudan had a President, Ethiopia had no PM).
  - Success: Clarified whether a Prime Minister position existed in that country at the time.

## Goal 3: Identify the specific individual who served as Prime Minister of that place in April 1977
- Path 3.1: If the place is India, search for “Prime Minister of India April 1977” and cross-reference with historical election dates (e.g., Morarji Desai took office on 24 March 1977).

- Path 3.3: If the place is a country with a different title (e.g., Sudan or Ethiopia), search for “head of state [country] April 1977” and check if a Prime Minister existed or if the leader held another title (e.g., President, Chairman). If no Prime Minister, this path would need reconsideration of the place name.
